metadata.geography: Virginia (WIN) - selected counties: Loudoun, Fairfax, Stafford, Fauquier, Spotsylvania
metadata.source: Primary sources: county agenda/packet PDF(s) (Laserfiche/CivicEngage) collected Aug 2025–Feb 2026
metadata.notes: This layer currently contains only 1 ingested WIN-side event (Loudoun BOS packet). Legistar endpoints tested for Loudoun returned 'Invalid parameters!' (blocked). Remaining counties not yet ingested in this pass. Added Loudoun BOS items (Sep–Nov 2025) focusing on data centers, substations, eminent domain/ROW, roadway projects, stormwater standards, solar exemption, and Runway 30 noise mitigation. Added 21 Loudoun BOS packet-derived WIN events for Dec 2025–Feb 2026 (contracts, transit items, stormwater MOAs, CTP CPAM, electrical infrastructure CPAM, easements).

properties.summary: BOS packet item proposes conveying easements to Dominion Energy Virginia and Comcast to serve Town of Purcellville water storage tank.
properties.quote: PURPOSE: The Town of Purcellville (Town) requests the conveyance of two easements on County-owned property (PIN 522-29-5928-002) to Dominion Energy Virginia and Comcast Cable Communications Management, LLC for the purpose of serving the Town’s water storage tank located at 300 Centreville Farm Drive, Purcellville, VA (PIN 522-30-6374-000).
properties.source_doc_title: Loudoun County BOS Business Meeting Packet - Item 10 (Dominion/Comcast Easements)
properties.operator: Virginia Electric and Power Company d.b.a. Dominion Energy Virginia
properties.project_name: Purcellville water storage tank service easements (Dominion Energy + Comcast)

properties.summary: Public hearing item proposes granting a 35-foot wide access easement to Loudoun Water through Lucketts Community Park.
properties.quote: PURPOSE: The purpose of this item is to hold a public hearing for the Board of Supervisors (Board) to consider granting a 35-foot wide access easement to Loudoun Water through and across a portion of County-owned property known as the Lucketts Community Park.

properties.operator: Loudoun County Sanitation Authority d.b.a. Loudoun Water
properties.project_name: Lucketts Community Park access easement to Loudoun Water

properties.summary: Public hearing item proposes assigning a sanitary sewer easement to Loudoun Water.

properties.summary: Public hearing item proposes authorizing eminent domain to acquire right-of-way and easements for the Evergreen Mills Road realignment project.
properties.quote: PURPOSE: The purpose of this item is to hold a public hearing and receive public comment for the proposed adoption of a resolution by the Board of Supervisors (Board) authorizing the use of eminent domain (condemnation) and Certificate of Take/Certificate of Deposit (or “quick take” proceedings) to acquire the right-of-way and easements necessary for the Evergreen Mills Road

properties.operator: Loudoun County Board of Supervisors
properties.project_name: Evergreen Mills Road realignment at Reservoir and Watson Roads (ROW/easements acquisition)

properties.summary: TLUC report seeks Board approval of Phase 2 scope for Data Center Standards and Locations CPAM/ZOAM, including clarifying policy on data center encroachment into Transition Policy Area place types.
properties.quote: PURPOSE: The purpose of this item is to: 1) update the Board of Supervisors (Board) on the Transportation and Land Use Committee’s (TLUC) discussion and recommendations regarding Phase 2 of the Data Center Standards and Locations Comprehensive Plan Amendment (CPAM)-2025-0001 and Zoning Ordinance Amendment (ZOAM)-2025-0001 (Phase 2), and 2) seek Board approval of the proposed scope outlined in the Project Plan (Attachment 1).

properties.operator: Loudoun County Department of Planning and Zoning
properties.project_name: CPAM-2025-0001 & ZOAM-2025-0001 Data Center Standards and Locations (Phase 2)
properties.geocode_query: Loudoun County Government Center, Leesburg, VA

properties.summary: Fairfax materials outline data-center-specific criteria for rezonings/special exceptions, including energy/water/air/noise topics.
properties.quote: A new criteria section provides guidance specific to data center development. These criteria build upon the January 2024 data center report and subsequent adopted Zoning Ordinance Amendment... and would apply to data centers seeking rezoning and/or special exception approval. These criteria include: Land Use and Site Design; Building Design; Green Building; Energy Demand; Water Demand; Water Quality; Air Quality and Noise.
properties.source_doc_title: Fairfax County BOS Board Package
properties.project_name: Countywide data center criteria
properties.geocode_query: Fairfax County Government Center, Fairfax, VA

properties.summary: Fairfax data center criteria include guidance on proximity to substations/transmission and undergrounding transmission lines where practicable.
properties.quote: Data centers proposals should provide the distance from existing substations and transmission lines... If a substation is proposed on the same site as the data center it is serving, it should be located or designed to minimize visibility... Transmission lines should be placed underground to the extent practicable.
properties.source_doc_title: Fairfax County BOS Board Package
properties.project_name: Data center grid siting guidance
properties.geocode_query: Fairfax County Government Center, Fairfax, VA

properties.summary: Fairfax planning materials flag a critical sewer capacity issue tied to pump stations operating at maximum capacity in a study area.
properties.quote: A critical sewer capacity issue given that the sites are served by two pump stations which are currently operating at maximum capacity and do not currently have sufficient capacity to accommodate flows from either of the proposals.
properties.source_doc_title: Fairfax County BOS Board Package
properties.project_name: Fairfax Center Area sewer capacity

properties.summary: Fairfax item notes use of quick-take eminent domain powers may be necessary to keep a construction project on schedule due to stalled acquisitions.
properties.quote: Negotiations are in progress... however, because resolution of these acquisitions is not imminent, it may become necessary for the Board to utilize quick-take eminent domain powers to commence construction of this project on schedule.
properties.source_doc_title: Fairfax County BOS Board Package
properties.project_name: ROW acquisitions (quick-take authority)

properties.summary: Fairfax environment element update work references research papers including stormwater management policies and green building policies.
properties.quote: Staff... produced a series of papers on Conservation Corridors, Green Building Policies, and Stormwater Management Policies... Staff therefore focused on improving the element and alignment with other adopted county plans.
properties.source_doc_title: Fairfax County BOS Board Package
properties.project_name: Environment element update
properties.geocode_query: Fairfax County Government Center, Fairfax, VA

properties.summary: Board adopted the updated 2025 Countywide Flood Risk Reduction Policy, establishing a framework for prioritizing flood risk projects/programs and addressing climate-adjusted 100-year storm risks.
properties.quote:
Staff recommend that the Board of Supervisors (Board) adopt the 2025 Countywide
Flood Risk Reduction Policy which provides a strategy for managing flood risk,
establishes a level of service, outlines objectives of land development regulations,
provides a revised prioritization of capital projects, and provides guidance for flood risk
programs.
properties.source_doc_title: Fairfax County Board of Supervisors - October 14, 2025 Final Board Package
properties.operator: Fairfax County Department of Public Works and Environmental Services (DPWES)
properties.project_name: 2025 Countywide Flood Risk Reduction Policy

properties.summary: Plan Amendment 2023-I-3J (Prosperity Business Campus) proposes adding a Comprehensive Plan option for residential mixed-use redevelopment up to 1.35 FAR (up to ~2,173 dwelling units) along Prosperity Avenue in the Merrifield Suburban Center.
properties.quote:
Plan Amendment (PA) 2023-I-3J proposes to amend the Comprehensive Plan
recommendations for a portion of Land Unit D in the Merrifield Suburban Center...
on Prosperity Avenue, by adding a Plan option for residential mixed-use redevelopment
up to an intensity of 1.35 floor area ratio (FAR)... The development potential could yield up to 2,173 dwelling units...
properties.source_doc_title: Fairfax County Board of Supervisors - November 18, 2025 Final Board Package (Final II)
properties.operator: Fairfax County Department of Planning and Development (DPD)
properties.project_name: SSPA-2023-I-3J Prosperity Business Campus (Merrifield Suburban Center) Comprehensive Plan Amendment
